title: ricinocarpus capitatus
intro: Ricinocarpus capitatus, also known as the cluster fig, is a plant species belonging to the family Celastraceae, native to tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The medicinal properties of Ricinocarpus capitatus have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of the plant include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. Ricinocarpus capitatus is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in topical applications for skin conditions and wounds.
